competitors.title: CLAUDE.md Examples and Best Practices 2026 | Claude Code Config Guide
key_points: Layered discovery system: managed policy > user global > project root > local project > parent dirs > subdirectories
Parent CLAUDE.md files load at launch, subdirectory files load on demand (important for monorepos)
200 recommended max lines, 5 max @import recursion depth, 200K context window
.claude/rules/ with paths frontmatter for path-scoped rules (load on demand vs at launch)
@import syntax for inline file expansion with 5-hop recursion limit
CLAUDE.md instructions are advisory (not deterministic); use hooks for guaranteed execution
Comparison table: CLAUDE.md vs AGENTS.md vs .cursorrules/.mdc vs GEMINI.md with discovery, scope, limits
6 real-world examples: minimal project, monorepo, Next.js app, Python ML, infrastructure/DevOps, hooks integration
CLAUDE.md fully survives compaction (re-read from disk); conversation-only instructions do not
Auto memory (MEMORY.md) vs CLAUDE.md: you write CLAUDE.md, Claude writes auto memory; auto memory first 200 lines only
Common mistakes: duplicating what Claude knows, style enforcement in CLAUDE.md instead of hooks, growing without pruning, secrets in CLAUDE.md, conflicting instructions across files
Prompt caching: CLAUDE.md is part of cached system prompt prefix, 90% cheaper on subsequent turns

competitors.title: How to Write a CLAUDE.md File: The Complete Guide for 2026
key_points: CLAUDE.md is the single most impactful thing for Claude Code users — 'the system prompt for your codebase'
7-section structure: Project Overview, Code Style/Conventions, Architecture/Patterns, Commands, What NOT to Do, File Organization, Testing Expectations
Negative instructions ('Do NOT') are surprisingly effective — telling Claude what to avoid is often more impactful than what to do
Real-world examples from Vercel Next.js (138K stars), LangChain (128K stars), Excalidraw (118K stars), Deno (106K stars), Zed Editor (76K stars)
Best practices: be specific not abstract, keep under 500 lines, use code examples (5-line example worth 50 lines of description), update regularly, test rules after changes
CLAUDE.md vs .cursorrules vs AGENTS.md vs .github/copilot-instructions.md — formats are similar enough to maintain one source of truth
Getting started: 1-paragraph project description + 5 conventions + 3 things never to do + common commands = enough for noticeable improvement
March 2026, from The Prompt Shelf (community CLAUDE.md collection)
